Operation 'DeleteIpsFromIpPool' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description has clarity issues and lacks important details for AI agent interpretation. The phrase 'All IPs associated with the Pool will be unassigned from the deleted Pool' is confusing—it's unclear whether the pool is being deleted or just modified. The improved version clarifies: (1) what inputs are required (pool ID and IP list), (2) what the operation returns, (3) the exact side effects (unassignment vs. deletion), and (4) edge case handling (non-assigned IPs). The summary is also more action-oriented and uses consistent terminology with the API domain.

data.current_summary: Delete a Batch of IPs from an IP Pool
data.current_description: This operation removes a batch of IPs from an IP Pool. All IPs associated with the Pool will be unassigned from the deleted Pool. However, this operation does not remove the IPs from your account.
data.summary_suggestion: Remove multiple IP addresses from an IP Pool
data.description_suggestion: Removes a batch of IP addresses from a specified IP Pool. The IPs will be unassigned from the pool but remain active in your account. Requires the pool ID and a list of IP addresses to remove. Returns a success response confirming the removal. IPs that are not currently assigned to the pool will be ignored. This operation does not delete the IPs from your account—only their association with the pool is removed.
data.reasoning: The current description has clarity issues and lacks important details for AI agent interpretation. The phrase 'All IPs associated with the Pool will be unassigned from the deleted Pool' is confusing—it's unclear whether the pool is being deleted or just modified. The improved version clarifies: (1) what inputs are required (pool ID and IP list), (2) what the operation returns, (3) the exact side effects (unassignment vs. deletion), and (4) edge case handling (non-assigned IPs). The summary is also more action-oriented and uses consistent terminology with the API domain.

message: Operation 'ListIpPool' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary uses 'GET' which is HTTP-method terminology rather than action-oriented language. Improved summary uses clearer action verb 'List' and specifies the resource being retrieved. data.current_summary: GET all IP Pools that have Associated IPs
data.current_description:
This operation returns a list of your IP Pools and a sample of each Pools' associated IP addresses.

A maximum of 10 IPs will be returned per IP Pool by default. To retrieve additional IP addresses associated with a Pool, use the "Get IPs Assigned to an IP Pool" operation. Each user may have a maximum of 100 IP Pools.
data.summary_suggestion: List all IP Pools with associated IP addresses
data.reasoning: Summary uses 'GET' which is HTTP-method terminology rather than action-oriented language. Improved summary uses clearer action verb 'List' and specifies the resource being retrieved. Description is complete with pagination details, sampling behavior, and cross-reference to related operation.

data.current_summary: Get Details for an IP Pool
data.current_description:
This operation will return the details for a specified IP Pool, including the Pool's name, ID, a sample list of the IPs associated with the Pool, and the total number of IPs belonging to the Pool.

A maximum of 10 IPs will be returned per IP Pool by default. To retrieve additional IP addresses associated with a Pool, use the "Get IPs Assigned to an IP Pool" operation.
data.description_suggestion: This operation retrieves details for a specified IP Pool, including the Pool's name, ID, a sample list of the IPs associated with the Pool (maximum 10 by default), and the total count of IPs belonging to the Pool. Returns a 200 response with the IP Pool object. To retrieve all IP addresses associated with a Pool, use the "Get IPs Assigned to an IP Pool" operation.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks explicit mention of the response format and HTTP status code. Enhanced description clarifies the response structure and status code for better AI agent understanding of what to expect.

data.current_description: This operation deletes an IP Pool and unassigns all IP addresses associated with the Pool. IP addresses associated with the deleted Pool will remain in your account.

data.current_summary: Add a Batch of IPs to an IP Pool
data.current_description: This operation appends a batch of IPs to an IP Pool. This operation requires all IP assignments to succeed. If any IP assignments fail, this endpoint will return an error.

message: Operation 'ListIp' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_context. The description mentions limitations but lacks clarity on pagination mechanics and filtering capabilities. Added explicit information about pagination parameters, filtering options, and clarified the mutual exclusivity constraint for better AI agent understanding of available query options and response structure.

data.current_summary: Get a List of all IP Addresses on your Account
data.current_description:
This operation returns a list of all IP addresses associated with your account. A sample of IP details is returned with each IP, including which Pools the IP is associated with, whether the IP is set to warm up automatically, and when the IP was last updated.

### Limitations

The `is_parent_assigned` parameter and `pool` parameter cannot be used at the same time. By definition, an IP cannot be assigned to a Pool if it is not first enabled. You can use either the `before_key` or `after_key` in combination with the `limit` parameter to iterate through paginated results but not both.
data.description_suggestion: This operation returns a paginated list of all IP addresses associated with your account. Each IP includes details such as Pool associations, warm-up status, enabled/disabled state, parent account assignment status, and modification timestamps. Supports filtering by Pool, parent assignment status, and enabled status. Pagination is supported via `before_key` or `after_key` parameters (not both). Note: `is_parent_assigned` and `pool` parameters cannot be used simultaneously, and IPs must be enabled before Pool assignment.
data.reasoning: The description mentions limitations but lacks clarity on pagination mechanics and filtering capabilities. Added explicit information about pagination parameters, filtering options, and clarified the mutual exclusivity constraint for better AI agent understanding of available query options and response structure.

data.current_summary: Add a Twilio SendGrid IP Address
data.current_description: This operation adds a Twilio SendGrid IP address to your account. You can also assign up to 100 Subusers to the IP address at creation.
data.description_suggestion: This operation adds a new IP address to your account and optionally assigns up to 100 Subusers to it during creation. The IP is added to your account inventory and becomes available for use in sending campaigns. Returns the newly created IP address object with its details including IP address, enabled status, and assigned Subuser list. The IP will be in a disabled state by default and must be explicitly enabled before use.
data.reasoning: Missing information about the return value and initial state of the created IP. Added details about the response object, default disabled state, and that the IP must be enabled before use to help AI agents understand the complete lifecycle and what to expect after creation.

data.current_summary: Update Details for an IP Address
data.current_description: This operation updates an IP address's settings, including whether the IP is set to warm up automatically, if the IP is  assigned by a parent account, and whether the IP is enabled or disabled. The request body must include at least one of the `is_auto_warmup`, `is_parent_assigned`, or `is_enabled` fields.
data.description_suggestion: This operation updates an IP address's configuration settings including warm-up automation, parent account assignment, and enabled/disabled status. At least one of the following fields must be provided in the request: `is_auto_warmup`, `is_parent_assigned`, or `is_enabled`. Partial updates are supported. Returns the updated IP address object with all current settings. Changes take effect immediately.
data.reasoning: Missing information about the response and when changes take effect. Added details about partial update support, return value, and immediate effect of changes to help AI agents understand the operation's scope and side effects.

data.current_description: This operation removes a batch of Subusers from a specified IP address.
